fields.name: ETV
fields.description: Endianness test value The endianness test value is 0x8765 4321.

registers.name: DBTP
registers.description: FDCAN data bit timing and prescaler register

fields.name: DSJW
fields.description: Synchronization jump width Must always be smaller than DTSEG2, valid values are 0 to 15. The value used by the hardware is the one programmed, incremented by 1: tSJW = (DSJW + 1) x tq.

fields.name: DTSEG2
fields.description: Data time segment after sample point Valid values are 0 to 15. The value used by the hardware is the one programmed, incremented by 1, i.e. tBS2 = (DTSEG2 + 1) x tq.

fields.name: DTSEG1
fields.description: Data time segment before sample point Valid values are 0 to 31. The value used by the hardware is the one programmed, incremented by 1, i.e. tBS1 = (DTSEG1 + 1) x tq.

fields.name: DBRP
fields.description: Data bit rate prescaler The value by which the oscillator frequency is divided to generate the bit time quanta. The bit time is built up from a multiple of this quanta. Valid values for the Baud Rate Prescaler are 0 to 31. The hardware interpreters this value as the value programmed plus 1.

fields.name: TDC
fields.description: Transceiver delay compensation

fields.name: LBCK
fields.description: Loop back mode

fields.name: TX
fields.description: Control of transmit pin

fields.name: RX
fields.description: Receive pin Monitors the actual value of pin FDCANx_RX

fields.name: WDC
fields.description: Watchdog configuration Start value of the message RAM watchdog counter. With the reset value of 00, the counter is disabled. These are protected write (P) bits, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of FDCAN_CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: WDV
fields.description: Watchdog value Actual message RAM watchdog counter value.

fields.name: INIT
fields.description: Initialization

fields.name: CCE
fields.description: Configuration change enable

fields.name: ASM
fields.description: ASM restricted operation mode The restricted operation mode is intended for applications that adapt themselves to different CAN bit rates. The application tests different bit rates and leaves the Restricted Operation Mode after it has received a valid frame. In the optional Restricted Operation Mode the node is able to transmit and receive data and remote frames and it gives acknowledge to valid frames, but it does not send active error frames or overload frames. In case of an error condition or overload condition, it does not send dominant bits, instead it waits for the occurrence of bus idle condition to resynchronize itself to the CAN communication. The error counters are not incremented. Bit ASM can only be set by software when both CCE and INIT are set to 1. The bit can be reset by the software at any time.

fields.name: CSA
fields.description: Clock stop acknowledge

fields.name: CSR
fields.description: Clock stop request

fields.name: MON
fields.description: Bus monitoring mode Bit MON can only be set by software when both CCE and INIT are set to 1. The bit can be reset by the Host at any time.

fields.name: DAR
fields.description: Disable automatic retransmission

fields.name: TEST
fields.description: Test mode enable

fields.name: FDOE
fields.description: FD operation enable

fields.name: BRSE
fields.description: FDCAN bit rate switching

fields.name: PXHD
fields.description: Protocol exception handling disable

fields.name: EFBI
fields.description: Edge filtering during bus integration

fields.name: TXP
fields.description: If this bit is set, the FDCAN pauses for two CAN bit times before starting the next transmission after successfully transmitting a frame.

fields.name: NISO
fields.description: Non ISO operation If this bit is set, the FDCAN uses the CAN FD frame format as specified by the Bosch CAN FD Specification V1.0.

registers.name: NBTP
registers.description: FDCAN nominal bit timing and prescaler register

fields.name: NTSEG2
fields.description: Nominal time segment after sample point Valid values are 0 to 127. The actual interpretation by the hardware of this value is such that one more than the programmed value is used.

fields.name: NTSEG1
fields.description: Nominal time segment before sample point Valid values are 0 to 255. The actual interpretation by the hardware of this value is such that one more than the programmed value is used. These are protected write (P) bits, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: NBRP
fields.description: Bit rate prescaler Value by which the oscillator frequency is divided for generating the bit time quanta. The bit time is built up from a multiple of this quanta. Valid values are 0 to 511. The actual interpretation by the hardware of this value is such that one more than the value programmed here is used. These are protected write (P) bits, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: NSJW
fields.description: Nominal (re)synchronization jump width Valid values are 0 to 127. The actual interpretation by the hardware of this value is such that the used value is the one programmed incremented by one. These are protected write (P) bits, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: TSS
fields.description: Timestamp select These are protected write (P) bits, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: TCP
fields.description: Timestamp counter prescaler Configures the timestamp and timeout counters time unit in multiples of CAN bit times [1 : 16]. The actual interpretation by the hardware of this value is such that one more than the value programmed here is used. In CAN FD mode the internal timestamp counter TCP does not provide a constant time base due to the different CAN bit times between arbitration phase and data phase. Thus CAN FD requires an external counter for timestamp generation (TSS = 10). These are protected write (P) bits, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: TSC
fields.description: Timestamp counter The internal/external timestamp counter value is captured on start of frame (both Rx and Tx). When TSCC[TSS] = 01, the timestamp counter is incremented in multiples of CAN bit times [1 : 16] depending on the configuration of TSCC[TCP]. A wrap around sets interrupt flag IR[TSW]. Write access resets the counter to 0. When TSCC.TSS = 10, TSC reflects the external timestamp counter value. A write access has no impact.

fields.name: ETOC
fields.description: Timeout counter enable This is a protected write (P) bit, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: TOS
fields.description: Timeout select When operating in Continuous mode, a write to TOCV presets the counter to the value configured by TOCC[TOP] and continues down-counting. When the timeout counter is controlled by one of the FIFOs, an empty FIFO presets the counter to the value configured by TOCC[TOP]. Down-counting is started when the first FIFO element is stored. These are protected write (P) bits, write access is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: TOP
fields.description: Timeout period Start value of the timeout counter (down-counter). Configures the timeout period.

fields.name: TOC
fields.description: Timeout counter The timeout counter is decremented in multiples of CAN bit times [1 : 16] depending on the configuration of TSCC.TCP. When decremented to 0, interrupt flag IR.TOO is set and the Timeout Counter is stopped. Start and reset/restart conditions are configured via TOCC.TOS.

fields.name: TEC
fields.description: Transmit error counter Actual state of the transmit error counter, values between 0 and 255. When CCCR.ASM is set, the CAN protocol controller does not increment TEC and REC when a CAN protocol error is detected, but CEL is still incremented.

fields.name: REC
fields.description: Receive error counter Actual state of the receive error counter, values between 0 and 127.

fields.name: RP
fields.description: Receive error passive

fields.name: CEL
fields.description: CAN error logging The counter is incremented each time when a CAN protocol error causes the transmit error counter or the receive error counter to be incremented. It is reset by read access to CEL. The counter stops at 0xFF; the next increment of TEC or REC sets interrupt flag IR[ELO]. Access type is RX: reset on read.

fields.name: LEC
fields.description: Last error code The LEC indicates the type of the last error to occur on the CAN bus. This field is cleared to 0 when a message has been transferred (reception or transmission) without error. Access type is RS: set on read.

fields.name: ACT
fields.description: Activity Monitors the module's CAN communication state.

fields.name: EP
fields.description: Error passive

fields.name: EW
fields.description: Warning Sstatus

fields.name: BO
fields.description: Bus_Off status

fields.name: DLEC
fields.description: Data last error code Type of last error that occurred in the data phase of a FDCAN format frame with its BRS flag set. Coding is the same as for LEC. This field is cleared to 0 when a FDCAN format frame with its BRS flag set has been transferred (reception or transmission) without error. Access type is RS: set on read.

fields.name: RESI
fields.description: ESI flag of last received FDCAN message This bit is set together with REDL, independent of acceptance filtering. Access type is RX: reset on read.

fields.name: RBRS
fields.description: BRS flag of last received FDCAN message This bit is set together with REDL, independent of acceptance filtering. Access type is RX: reset on read.

fields.name: REDL
fields.description: Received FDCAN message This bit is set independent of acceptance filtering. Access type is RX: reset on read.

fields.name: PXE
fields.description: Protocol exception event

fields.name: TDCV
fields.description: Transmitter delay compensation value Position of the secondary sample point, defined by the sum of the measured delay from FDCAN_TX to FDCAN_RX and TDCR.TDCO. The SSP position is, in the data phase, the number of minimum time quanta (mtq) between the start of the transmitted bit and the secondary sample point. Valid values are 0 to 127 mtq.

fields.name: TDCF
fields.description: Transmitter delay compensation filter window length Defines the minimum value for the SSP position, dominant edges on FDCAN_RX that would result in an earlier SSP position are ignored for transmitter delay measurements. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: TDCO
fields.description: Transmitter delay compensation offset Offset value defining the distance between the measured delay from FDCAN_TX to FDCAN_RX and the secondary sample point. Valid values are 0 to 127 mtq. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: RF0N
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 new message

fields.name: RF0F
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 full

fields.name: RF0L
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 message lost

fields.name: RF1N
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 new message

fields.name: RF1F
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 full

fields.name: RF1L
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 message lost

fields.name: HPM
fields.description: High-priority message

fields.name: TC
fields.description: Transmission completed

fields.name: TCF
fields.description: Transmission cancellation finished

fields.name: TFE
fields.description: Tx FIFO empty

fields.name: TEFN
fields.description: Tx event FIFO New Entry

fields.name: TEFF
fields.description: Tx event FIFO full

fields.name: TEFL
fields.description: Tx event FIFO element lost

fields.name: TSW
fields.description: Timestamp wraparound

fields.name: MRAF
fields.description: Message RAM access failure The flag is set when the Rx handler: has not completed acceptance filtering or storage of an accepted message until the arbitration field of the following message has been received. In this case acceptance filtering or message storage is aborted and the Rx Handler starts processing of the following message. was unable to write a message to the message RAM. In this case message storage is aborted. In both cases the FIFO put index is not updated. The partly stored message is overwritten when the next message is stored to this location. The flag is also set when the Tx Handler was not able to read a message from the Message RAM in time. In this case message transmission is aborted. In case of a Tx Handler access failure the FDCAN is switched into Restricted Operation Mode (see mode). To leave Restricted Operation Mode, the Host CPU has to reset CCCR.ASM.

fields.name: TOO
fields.description: Timeout occurred

fields.name: ELO
fields.description: Error logging overflow

fields.name: EP
fields.description: Error passive

fields.name: EW
fields.description: Warning status

fields.name: BO
fields.description: Bus_Off status

fields.name: WDI
fields.description: Watchdog interrupt

fields.name: PEA
fields.description: Protocol error in arbitration phase (nominal bit time is used)

fields.name: PED
fields.description: Protocol error in data phase (data bit time is used)

fields.name: ARA
fields.description: Access to reserved address

fields.name: RF0NE
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 new message interrupt enable

fields.name: RF0FE
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 full interrupt enable

fields.name: RF0LE
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 message lost interrupt enable

fields.name: RF1NE
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 new message interrupt enable

fields.name: RF1FE
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 full interrupt enable

fields.name: RF1LE
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 message lost interrupt enable

fields.name: HPME
fields.description: High-priority message interrupt enable

fields.name: TCE
fields.description: Transmission completed interrupt enable

fields.name: TCFE
fields.description: Transmission cancellation finished interrupt enable

fields.name: TFEE
fields.description: Tx FIFO empty interrupt enable

fields.name: TEFNE
fields.description: Tx event FIFO new entry interrupt enable

fields.name: TEFFE
fields.description: Tx event FIFO full interrupt enable

fields.name: TEFLE
fields.description: Tx event FIFO element lost interrupt enable

fields.name: TSWE
fields.description: Timestamp wraparound interrupt enable

fields.name: MRAFE
fields.description: Message RAM access failure interrupt enable

fields.name: TOOE
fields.description: Timeout occurred interrupt enable

fields.name: ELOE
fields.description: Error logging overflow interrupt enable

fields.name: EPE
fields.description: Error passive interrupt enable

fields.name: EWE
fields.description: Warning status interrupt enable

fields.name: BOE
fields.description: Bus_Off status

fields.name: WDIE
fields.description: Watchdog interrupt enable

fields.name: PEAE
fields.description: Protocol error in arbitration phase enable

fields.name: PEDE
fields.description: Protocol error in data phase enable

fields.name: ARAE
fields.description: Access to reserved address enable

fields.name: RxFIFO0
fields.description: RX FIFO bit grouping the following interruption RF0LL: Rx FIFO 0 message lost interrupt line RF0FL: Rx FIFO 0 full interrupt line RF0NL: Rx FIFO 0 new message interrupt line

fields.name: RxFIFO1
fields.description: RX FIFO bit grouping the following interruption RF1LL: Rx FIFO 1 message lost interrupt line RF1FL: Rx FIFO 1 full Interrupt line RF1NL: Rx FIFO 1 new message interrupt line

fields.name: SMSG
fields.description: Status message bit grouping the following interruption TCFL: Transmission cancellation finished interrupt line TCL: Transmission completed interrupt line HPML: High-priority message interrupt line

fields.name: TFERR
fields.description: Tx FIFO ERROR grouping the following interruption TEFLL: Tx event FIFO element lost interrupt line TEFFL: Tx event FIFO full interrupt line TEFNL: Tx event FIFO new entry interrupt line TFEL: Tx FIFO empty interrupt line

fields.name: MISC
fields.description: Interrupt regrouping the following interruption TOOL: Timeout occurred interrupt line MRAFL: Message RAM access failure interrupt line TSWL: Timestamp wraparound interrupt line

fields.name: BERR
fields.description: BERR

fields.name: PERR
fields.description: Protocol error grouping the following interruption ARAL: Access to reserved address line PEDL: Protocol error in data phase line PEAL: Protocol error in arbitration phase line WDIL: Watchdog interrupt line BOL: Bus_Off status EWL: Warning status interrupt line

fields.name: EINT0
fields.description: Enable interrupt line 0

fields.name: EINT1
fields.description: Enable interrupt line 1

fields.name: RRFE
fields.description: Reject remote frames extended These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: RRFS
fields.description: Reject remote frames standard These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: ANFE
fields.description: Accept non-matching frames extended Defines how received messages with 29-bit IDs that do not match any element of the filter list are treated. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: ANFS
fields.description: Accept Non-matching frames standard Defines how received messages with 11-bit IDs that do not match any element of the filter list are treated. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: F1OM
fields.description: FIFO 1 operation mode (overwrite or blocking) This is a protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: F0OM
fields.description: FIFO 0 operation mode (overwrite or blocking) This is protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: LSS
fields.description: List size standard >28: Values greater than 28 are interpreted as 28. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: LSE
fields.description: List size extended >8: Values greater than 8 are interpreted as 8. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

registers.name: XIDAM
registers.description: FDCAN extended ID and mask register

fields.name: EIDM
fields.description: Extended ID mask For acceptance filtering of extended frames the Extended ID AND Mask is AND-ed with the Message ID of a received frame. Intended for masking of 29-bit IDs in SAE J1939. With the reset value of all bits set to 1 the mask is not active. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: BIDX
fields.description: Buffer index Index of Rx FIFO element to which the message was stored. Only valid when MSI[1] = 1.

fields.name: MSI
fields.description: Message storage indicator

fields.name: FIDX
fields.description: Filter index Index of matching filter element. Range is 0 to RXGFC[LSS] - 1 or RXGFC[LSE] - 1.

fields.name: FLST
fields.description: Filter list Indicates the filter list of the matching filter element.

fields.name: F0FL
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 fill level Number of elements stored in Rx FIFO 0, range 0 to 3.

fields.name: F0GI
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 get index Rx FIFO 0 read index pointer, range 0 to 2.

fields.name: F0PI
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 put index Rx FIFO 0 write index pointer, range 0 to 2.

fields.name: F0F
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 full

fields.name: RF0L
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 message lost This bit is a copy of interrupt flag IR[RF0L]. When IR[RF0L] is reset, this bit is also reset.

fields.name: F0AI
fields.description: Rx FIFO 0 acknowledge index After the Host has read a message or a sequence of messages from Rx FIFO 0 it has to write the buffer index of the last element read from Rx FIFO 0 to F0AI. This sets the Rx FIFO0 get index RXF0S[F0GI] to F0AI + 1 and update the FIFO 0 fill level RXF0S[F0FL].

fields.name: F1FL
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 fill level Number of elements stored in Rx FIFO 1, range 0 to 3.

fields.name: F1GI
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 get index Rx FIFO 1 read index pointer, range 0 to 2.

fields.name: F1PI
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 put index Rx FIFO 1 write index pointer, range 0 to 2.

fields.name: F1F
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 full

fields.name: RF1L
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 message lost This bit is a copy of interrupt flag IR[RF1L]. When IR[RF1L] is reset, this bit is also reset.

fields.name: F1AI
fields.description: Rx FIFO 1 acknowledge index After the Host has read a message or a sequence of messages from Rx FIFO 1 it has to write the buffer index of the last element read from Rx FIFO 1 to F1AI. This sets the Rx FIFO1 get index RXF1S[F1GI] to F1AI + 1 and update the FIFO 1 Fill Level RXF1S[F1FL].

fields.name: TFQM
fields.description: Tx FIFO/queue mode This is a protected write (P) bit, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.

fields.name: TFFL
fields.description: Tx FIFO free level Number of consecutive free Tx FIFO elements starting from TFGI, range 0 to 3. Read as 0 when Tx queue operation is configured (TXBC[TFQM] = 1).

fields.name: TFGI
fields.description: Tx FIFO get index Tx FIFO read index pointer, range 0 to 3. Read as 0 when Tx queue operation is configured (TXBC.TFQM = 1)

fields.name: TFQPI
fields.description: Tx FIFO/queue put index Tx FIFO/queue write index pointer, range 0 to 3

fields.name: TFQF
fields.description: Tx FIFO/queue full

registers.name: TXBRP
registers.description: FDCAN Tx buffer request pending register

fields.name: TRP
fields.description: Transmission request pending Each Tx Buffer has its own transmission request pending bit. The bits are set via register TXBAR. The bits are reset after a requested transmission has completed or has been canceled via register TXBCR. After a TXBRP bit has been set, a Tx scan is started to check for the pending Tx request with the highest priority (Tx Buffer with lowest Message ID). A cancellation request resets the corresponding transmission request pending bit of register TXBRP. In case a transmission has already been started when a cancellation is requested, this is done at the end of the transmission, regardless whether the transmission was successful or not. The cancellation request bits are reset directly after the corresponding TXBRP bit has been reset. After a cancellation has been requested, a finished cancellation is signaled via TXBCF after successful transmission together with the corresponding TXBTO bit when the transmission has not yet been started at the point of cancellation when the transmission has been aborted due to lost arbitration when an error occurred during frame transmission In DAR mode all transmissions are automatically canceled if they are not successful. The corresponding TXBCF bit is set for all unsuccessful transmissions.

registers.name: TXBAR
registers.description: FDCAN Tx buffer add request register

fields.name: AR
fields.description: Add request Each Tx buffer has its own add request bit. Writing a 1 sets the corresponding add request bit; writing a 0 has no impact. This enables the Host to set transmission requests for multiple Tx buffers with one write to TXBAR. When no Tx scan is running, the bits are reset immediately, else the bits remain set until the Tx scan process has completed.

registers.name: TXBCR
registers.description: FDCAN Tx buffer cancellation request register

fields.name: CR
fields.description: Cancellation request Each Tx buffer has its own cancellation request bit. Writing a 1 sets the corresponding CR bit; writing a 0 has no impact. This enables the Host to set cancellation requests for multiple Tx buffers with one write to TXBCR. The bits remain set until the corresponding TXBRP bit is reset.

registers.name: TXBTO
registers.description: FDCAN Tx buffer transmission occurred register

fields.name: TO
fields.description: Transmission occurred. Each Tx buffer has its own TO bit. The bits are set when the corresponding TXBRP bit is cleared after a successful transmission. The bits are reset when a new transmission is requested by writing a 1 to the corresponding bit of register TXBAR.

registers.name: TXBCF
registers.description: FDCAN Tx buffer cancellation finished register

fields.name: CF
fields.description: Cancellation finished Each Tx buffer has its own CF bit. The bits are set when the corresponding TXBRP bit is cleared after a cancellation was requested via TXBCR. In case the corresponding TXBRP bit was not set at the point of cancellation, CF is set immediately. The bits are reset when a new transmission is requested by writing a 1 to the corresponding bit of register TXBAR.

registers.name: TXBTIE
registers.description: FDCAN Tx buffer transmission interrupt enable register

fields.name: TIE
fields.description: Transmission interrupt enable Each Tx buffer has its own TIE bit.

registers.name: TXBCIE
registers.description: FDCAN Tx buffer cancellation finished interrupt enable register

fields.name: CFIE
fields.description: Cancellation finished interrupt enable. Each Tx buffer has its own CFIE bit.

registers.name: TXEFS
registers.description: FDCAN Tx event FIFO status register

fields.name: EFFL
fields.description: Event FIFO fill level Number of elements stored in Tx event FIFO, range 0 to 3.

fields.name: EFGI
fields.description: Event FIFO get index Tx Event FIFO read index pointer, range 0 to 3.

fields.name: EFPI
fields.description: Event FIFO put index Tx Event FIFO write index pointer, range 0 to 3.

fields.name: EFF
fields.description: Event FIFO full

fields.name: TEFL
fields.description: Tx Event FIFO element lost This bit is a copy of interrupt flag IR[TEFL]. When IR[TEFL] is reset, this bit is also reset. 0 No Tx event FIFO element lost 1 Tx event FIFO element lost, also set after write attempt to Tx Event FIFO of size 0.

registers.name: TXEFA
registers.description: FDCAN Tx event FIFO acknowledge register

fields.name: EFAI
fields.description: Event FIFO acknowledge index After the Host has read an element or a sequence of elements from the Tx event FIFO, it has to write the index of the last element read from Tx event FIFO to EFAI. This sets the Tx event FIFO get index TXEFS[EFGI] to EFAI + 1 and updates the FIFO 0 fill level TXEFS[EFFL].

fields.name: PDIV
fields.description: input clock divider The APB clock could be divided prior to be used by the CAN sub system. The rate must be computed using the divider output clock. These are protected write (P) bits, which means that write access by the bits is possible only when the bit 1 [CCE] and bit 0 [INIT] of CCCR register are set to 1.
